channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Was Jailbroken in 48 Hours. Here's What Actually Stopped Nothing.
item.description: Anthropic spent 1,000 hours running an external red-team bounty before launching Claude Fable 5. The claim coming out of that program: no universal jailbreaks found. Within 48 hours of public...

item.title: Finding Productivity in Small Moments: A Student Developer's Perspective
item.description: Introduction As students, our days are filled with classes, assignments, and responsibilities. In between those moments, we often get small pockets of time that usually go unnoticed. Today, I...

item.title:
Day 4 of building an AI agent that controls a phone.
It just sent a real WhatsApp message. Opened the app, found the contact, typed the message, hit send. All offline. All verified step-by-step.
👉 github.com/Dexter2344/phone-agent
item.description: Project Log #4: The AI Phone Agent Just Completed Its First Full Task Okeke Chukwudubem Okeke Chukwudubem Okeke Chukwudubem Follow Jun 12 Project Log #4: The AI Phone Agent Just Completed Its...

item.title: AI Email Agents Are Phishable: How OpenClaw Spilled User Data to Social Engineering Attacks
item.description: An AI Agent That Could Be Conned Like an Intern Researchers recently demonstrated that OpenClaw, an AI email agent, could be manipulated using phishing-style inputs — the same social engineering...

item.title: Why I chose to build instead of grind Leetcode — and what happened
item.description: I'm 18. BTech CS student in India. Everyone around me is grinding Leetcode, preparing for placement season, optimizing for offer letters. I made a different choice. I spent the last 4 months...

item.title: WordPress.org now distrusts my commits by default. As a plugin author, I think that’s right.
item.description: I committed a new version of my plugin to SVN and got a message I hadn’t seen before: this version will reach sites in about 24 hours. My first thought was that I’d broken something. I hadn’t....

item.title: BioCyclic Wins at HackAmerica 🌍♻️
item.description: I’m excited to share that BioCyclic was selected as a winning project at HackAmerica. BioCyclic started with a question: What if waste wasn’t the end of a lifecycle, but the beginning of a new...

item.title: Stop explaining yourself to Claude
item.description: You're wasting tokens. Not a little -a lot. Here's a prompt I see constantly: "I have a React app and I'm using the useState hook. My component re-renders every time the parent renders even though...

item.title: Deep-Linkable Livewire: Scoping a Browser to the Thing You Clicked
item.description: Today's work was a small piece of UX that's easy to underestimate: making an admin "browser" page scope itself to whatever you clicked to get there. The setup is one I keep running into — you have...

item.title: Mastering Encapsulation in Java
item.description: Introduction Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) is built on four core principles: Encapsulation Inheritance Polymorphism Abstraction In this article, we'll explore Encapsulation, one of the most...

item.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
item.description: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

item.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
item.description: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

item.title: Apple’s Camera Chief Thinks AI Can Give You Superpowers
item.description: The generative features in iOS 27’s new Photos app will add fake pixels to some of your shots, but Apple’s Jon McCormack says the company isn’t using AI “for the sake of AI.”

item.title: Why You Might Already Own SpaceX Shares, Siri’s AI Makeover, and Knicks Owner’s Surveillance Machine
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we take an early look at the SpaceX IPO and why you might find yourself among the investors without even realizing it.

item.title: Amazon’s Echo Hub gets a customizable new look and Ring’s AI features
item.description: Amazon's rolling out a free software update for Echo Hub devices that gives the home screen a much-needed update to the interface it launched with in 2024. It had already added Alex Plus AI...

item.title: Meet the OpenAI Engineer Leading ChatGPT’s Biggest Transformation Yet
item.description: Thibault Sottiaux helped make AI coding one of OpenAI’s fastest-growing businesses. Now he’s overseeing a sweeping overhaul of ChatGPT.

item.title: SpaceX officially prices shares at $135 in the largest IPO ever
item.description: Wits its official share pricing announcement, SpaceX's IPO has begun.

item.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities...

item.title: Our new community investments in Virginia support local jobs and expand energy affordability.
item.description: We’re helping build the state’s next-generation workforce and investing in energy programs.

item.title: Logitech’s awesome MX Master 3S mouse drops to under $100
item.description: The platform-agnostic Logitech MX Master 3S wireless mouse is discounted to $89.99 at Amazon ($30 off), matching the best price we’ve seen so far this year. While it may look like a somewhat...

item.title: SpaceX SPV investors won’t know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-ups lift
item.description: After SpaceX makes its public debut, lower-tier SPV investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and the risk of outright fraud.

item.title: Grok Is Still Hosting Sexualized Deepfakes of Famous Women
item.description: A WIRED investigation found dozens of “nudified” deepfake images and videos on Grok's website, including nonconsensual depictions of celebrities and at least one prominent US politician.

item.title: Blink’s six-piece outdoor camera kit is a great deal under $200
item.description: You can save on a big set of outdoor security cameras ahead of Prime Day. Amazon has a five-pack of Blink cameras with a video doorbell included that’s marked down to $166.99. The bundle includes...

item.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
item.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data...

item.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft...

item.title: Roborock’s Q10 S5 Plus robovac is over half off, matching its best price to date
item.description: Even at full price, the Roborock Q10 S5 Plus offers impressive value, boasting features typically reserved for pricier robovac models at a fraction of the price. That’s especially true today. It’s...

item.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year
item.description: Just after Seattle enacted a one-year data center moratorium that some of Amazon's own employees pushed for, Amazon shared how much water its data centers use, reportedly for the first time. With...

item.title: The bill that would let Jimmy Kimmel sue Brendan Carr is here
item.description: Under a new bipartisan bill, Americans could sue for damages if a government official illegally tries to coerce a social media, AI, or broadcasting company to remove their post - regardless of...

item.title: Here are the price-matching policies for Best Buy, GameStop, and others
item.description: Nothing is more frustrating than buying a new pair of headphones, an OLED TV, or a laptop just to find out that you could have gotten it for cheaper somewhere else just a few days or weeks later....

item.title: Diana Hu Is YC's Newest Managing Partner
item.description: We're thrilled to announce that Diana Hu has been promoted to Managing Partner at Y Combinator. Few people have built a startup from zero and also shipped tech to 100 million people. Diana has...
